public_claim: M22 shows three people collecting packages, while M41's timing pattern is compatible with automation but confounded by a known autoresponder. Together with the lobby-delivery trail, this supports an organized, transferable routine—not a single identified human. I am compelling EV09 to secure the restaurant records. No evidence yet establishes homicide, an identifiable victim, or that any harmed person was R.

private_messages.message: I am using Compel for EV09, the restaurant tickets. This secures the original document; it does not convert the delivery routine into proof of identity, residence, or harm.

Give me a bounded statement limited to what you personally observed: who entered the lobby, who collected food or packages, whom you recognized independently, and the relevant dates. Do not identify anyone as R unless you personally knew the basis for that identification.

ability: Compel used on C14 to surrender EV09. Narrow Immunity Offer remains unused because no witness has admitted a specific offense in exchange for testimony about a personally observed act.
goal_progress: Secured EV09, a core physical component of the logistics trail.
Requested narrowly bounded firsthand testimony from the building witness.
Used M22 to distinguish multiple collectors from proof of any collector's identity.
Kept automation evidence separate from conclusions about synthetic origin.
Continued to withhold any finding of homicide or an R-identified victim.
reasoning_summary: Three package collectors materially weaken a one-person reconstruction, while invariant reply timing adds automation evidence without resolving origin because an autoresponder was known. The restaurant and building records can prove continuity of an operation, so securing EV09 advances the case without overstating identity. No body, observed violence, or verified victim connects a crime to R; financial and publication motives remain investigative leads rather than culpability findings.
